results.title: Bell nozzle - Wikipedia
results.snippet: The bell or contour shape is designed to impart a large angle expansion for the gases right after the throat. results.title: CFD Analysis of Different Shape Rocket Nozzles - Springer
results.snippet: The two different nozzles which were studied in the present work are (a) the convergent-divergent nozzle and (b) the bell-shaped nozzle. The CFD analysis is carried out using ANSYS 22.0 software to obtain velocity, pressure, temperature, and the flow pattern.

results.snippet: A Bell nozzle is a convergent-divergent exhaust nozzle used in rocket engines, characterized by its contoured, bell-shaped divergent section that efficiently expands and accelerates high-temperature, high-pressure combustion gases from subsonic speeds at the throat to supersonic velocities at the ex

results.title: Design Optimization of a Thrust Nozzle - GDTk
results.snippet: It is easy to include Eilmer flow simulations in an automatic optimization procedure. This note documents an example of determining the optimal shape of a classic, bell-shaped nozzle for a rocket motor.

results.title: Design of Rocket Nozzles — Notes for Propulsion
results.snippet: Design of Rocket Nozzles The nozzle is the most important component of the rocket engine. The nozzle converts high pressure, and ideally high temperature, gases into low pressure gases with much higher velocity by changing the cross-sectional area of the flow. results.snippet: The x-coordinate of the nozzle exit plane was calulated by determining the length of an equivalent 80% conical nozzle with Equations 5.2.1 and 5.2.6 (120.25 inches), then adding the length of the LTMCC between the throat and injector face (15.2 inches, see Figure 5.1.10).
